int.description: Signed integer type, typically 32 bits, for whole number arithmetic

char.description: Single byte character type, typically 8 bits, used for ASCII characters and small integers

long.description: Extended signed integer type, at least 32 bits wide

void.description: Incomplete type representing the absence of a value, used for function return types and generic pointers

size_t.description: Unsigned integer type for object sizes and array indexing, guaranteed to hold any array index

FILE.description: Opaque structure representing a file stream for buffered I/O operations

NULL.description: Null pointer constant representing an invalid or uninitialized pointer value

bool.description: Boolean type holding true (1) or false (0) values, introduced in C99 via stdbool.h

ptrdiff_t.description: Signed integer type for pointer arithmetic differences

uint8_t.description: Unsigned 8-bit integer type with exact width

int32_t.description: Signed 32-bit integer type with exact width

uint64_t.description: Unsigned 64-bit integer type with exact width
